questTitle: P6 Math Revision: Percentage, Fraction & Ratio (Part 1)
subject: Mathematics
description: Challenge yourself with percentage, fraction and ratio problems! This worksheet covers converting between percentages and fractions, comparing quantities, and finding fractions of fractions. Read each question carefully.

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Percentage to Fraction Conversion
questions.question: Amy spent 60% of her money on Monday. How can 60% be expressed as a fraction in its simplest form?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Percentage to Fraction Conversion
questions.question: If Amy spent 3/5 of her money on Monday, what fraction of her original money did she have left?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Percentage to Fraction Conversion
questions.question: Amy has 2/5 of her money left after Monday. She then spends 1/4 of this remainder on Tuesday. What fraction of her ORIGINAL money does she spend on Tuesday?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Percentage to Fraction Conversion
questions.question: Amy spent 3/5 on Monday and 1/10 on Tuesday. What fraction of her original money has she spent so far?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 1: Percentage to Fraction Conversion - Test Question
questions.question: Ben spent 75% of his money on Saturday. He then spent 2/5 of the remainder on Sunday and the rest on Monday. What fraction of his money was spent on Monday?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Quantities Using Percentages
questions.question: 50% of Lisa's sweets equals 20% of Amy's sweets. If Lisa has 10 sweets, how many sweets represent 50% of Lisa's sweets?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Quantities Using Percentages
questions.question: 50% of Lisa's sweets equals 20% of Amy's sweets. If 50% of Lisa's sweets is 5 sweets, and this equals 20% of Amy's sweets, how many sweets does Amy have in total?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Quantities Using Percentages
questions.question: If Lisa has 10 sweets and Amy has 25 sweets, what is the ratio of Lisa's sweets to Amy's sweets in its simplest form?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Quantities Using Percentages
questions.question: The ratio of Lisa's sweets to Amy's sweets is 2 : 5. How many parts represent their combined total?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 2: Comparing Quantities Using Percentages - Test Question
questions.question: 40% of Maria's stickers equals 25% of Nina's stickers. What is the ratio of Maria's stickers to Nina's stickers?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Fraction of a Fraction
questions.question: In a room, 3/5 of the people are children and the rest are adults. What fraction of the people are adults?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Fraction of a Fraction
questions.question: Of the children, 40% are boys. How can 40% be expressed as a fraction?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Fraction of a Fraction
questions.question: Children make up 3/5 of the total people. Boys make up 2/5 of the children. What fraction of ALL the people are boys?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Fraction of a Fraction
questions.question: If 40% of the children are boys, what percentage of the children are girls?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 3: Fraction of a Fraction - Test Question
questions.question: At a party, 4/7 of the guests are adults and the rest are children. 75% of the children are girls. What fraction of ALL the guests are girls?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Fraction of Total - Books
questions.question: At a bookshop, 2/5 of the books are fiction. What fraction of the books are non-fiction?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Fraction of Total - Books
questions.question: 25% of the fiction books were sold. What fraction of the fiction books were sold?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Fraction of Total - Books
questions.question: Fiction books make up 2/5 of all books. If 1/4 of the fiction books were sold, what fraction of ALL the books were fiction books that were sold?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Fraction of Total - Books
questions.question: Non-fiction books make up 3/5 of all books. 40% of non-fiction books were sold. What fraction of ALL the books were non-fiction books that were sold?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 4: Fraction of Total - Books - Test Question
questions.question: At a library, 3/7 of the books are hardcover and the rest are paperback. 20% of the hardcover books and 50% of the paperback books were borrowed. If 260 books remain unborrowed, how many books were there at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution
questions.question: Peter, Mary and John shared some money. Peter received 40% of the money. What fraction of the total money did Peter receive?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution
questions.question: Peter received 2/5 of the total money. What fraction of the total money is the remainder?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution
questions.question: The remainder is 3/5 of the total. Mary received 2/3 of this remainder. What fraction of the TOTAL money did Mary receive?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution
questions.question: Peter received 2/5, Mary received 2/5. What fraction of the total money did John receive?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 5: Money Distribution - Test Question
questions.question: Alice, Bob and Carol shared some prize money. Alice received 25% of the money. Bob received 3/5 of the remainder. Carol received the rest, which was $90. How much prize money did they share altogether?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Ratio and Percentage Transfer
questions.question: The ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps is 5 : 7. How many parts does Ali have?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Ratio and Percentage Transfer
questions.question: Ali gives 20% of his stamps to Ben. What fraction of Ali's stamps does he give away?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Ratio and Percentage Transfer
questions.question: Ali has 5 parts and gives away 1/5 of his stamps. How many parts does Ali give to Ben?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Ratio and Percentage Transfer
questions.question: After the transfer, Ali has (5 - 1) = 4 parts and Ben has (7 + 1) = 8 parts. What is the new ratio of Ali's stamps to Ben's stamps in simplest form?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 6: Ratio and Percentage Transfer - Test Question
questions.question: The ratio of Chloe's beads to Devi's beads is 3 : 5. Chloe gives 25% of her beads to Devi. Devi then gives 24 beads back to Chloe so that they have the same number of beads. How many beads did Chloe have at first?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Composition
questions.question: 75% of the students in a class are present. What fraction of the class is present?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Composition
questions.question: Of the students present, 2/3 are girls. What fraction of the students present are boys?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Composition
questions.question: 3/4 of the class is present, and 2/3 of those present are girls. What fraction of the WHOLE class are girls who are present?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Composition
questions.question: 1/4 of the class is absent. 50% of those absent are boys. What fraction of the WHOLE class are boys who are absent?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 7: Class Composition - Test Question
questions.question: 80% of the students in a class are present. Of those present, 3/4 are girls. Of those absent, 75% are boys. If there are 15 more girls than boys in the class, how many students are in the class?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Ratio and Mixture
questions.question: A painter mixes red and blue paint in the ratio 2 : 3. What fraction of the mixture is red paint?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Ratio and Mixture
questions.question: The ratio of red to blue paint is 2 : 3. If the total mixture is 25 litres, how many litres of blue paint are there?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Ratio and Mixture
questions.question: A mixture has 10 litres of red and 15 litres of blue paint. If the painter adds 2 litres of red paint, what is the new amount of red paint?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Ratio and Mixture
questions.question: After adding 2 litres of red and 1 litre of blue, there are 12 litres of red and 16 litres of blue. What is the new ratio of red to blue paint in simplest form?

questions.problemSet: PROBLEM SET 8: Ratio and Mixture - Test Question
questions.question: A chef mixes oil and vinegar in the ratio 3 : 2. She then adds 4 litres of oil and 6 litres of vinegar. The new ratio of oil to vinegar becomes 1 : 1. How many litres of dressing did she have in the original mixture?
